notes: Items in the Pomegranate Hour suite — DATs, cassettes, floppies, scratch papers, props from the episodes. Items live in physical piles around the suite; their use is mostly at the CONSOLE (drop a cue, drop a take) or the PATCH BAY (route a cable to an episode). No bindle-construction analogue; the priestess wins by ADVANCING EPISODES + sitting with cast/crew, not by assembling an object.

dat_cue_14_draft_3.title: DAT · CUE 14 (DRAFT 3)
dat_cue_14_draft_3.flavor: Davey's hand. The label reads: please don't use this one. The 'please' is underlined. You use it.

hi8_young_elicia_96.title: Hi8 · E.T. → E.T. ('96)
hi8_young_elicia_96.flavor: You shot this in '96 as a joke during a long night in the cutting room. You looked into the lens and you told your future self what the show was going to be.

dat_hen_voicemail_02.title: DAT · Hen Voicemail · 2002
dat_hen_voicemail_02.flavor: Hen's voice from '02 — the ADR session he was supposed to fly back for. He didn't. The voicemail apologizes for six minutes before he hangs up.

master_ep3_v3.title: MASTER · EP. 3 · V3 · LOCKED
master_ep3_v3.flavor: The locked cut of Six Doors Down. The label is gold-foiled because Marisol did it as a joke in '98. It is the only gold-foil label in the suite.

dat_anya_1997.title: DAT · PRIVATE / ANYA / 1997
dat_anya_1997.flavor: From the locked drawer of nineteen. The star is drawn next to the name in your hand. The drawer has a key. The key is in your jewelry box at home, which is upstairs.

tape_apologist_19.title: Tape · The Apologist · 19
tape_apologist_19.flavor: One of the nineteen tapes in the locked drawer. The drawer has remembered itself locked for nine years.

pomegranate_half_eaten.title: Pomegranate · Half-Eaten
pomegranate_half_eaten.flavor: On the desk since '99. The seeds are dust now. You will not throw it out.

soundstage_receipt_97.title: Receipt · Soundstage · 86 MIN · MAR '97
soundstage_receipt_97.flavor: The block you paid for and didn't use. The handwriting on the back, in pencil, says: SAVE — Marisol & I will go back to this.

sharpie_in_elicias_hand.title: Sharpie · Black · Worn
sharpie_in_elicias_hand.flavor: The label-maker. You have used this sharpie to label twelve hundred cassettes. The tip is worn flat the way only your sharpies wear flat.

marisol_overnight_bag.title: Marisol's Overnight Bag
marisol_overnight_bag.flavor: Canvas, with a tag from a Mexican airline she flew in '11. Empty except for a paperback and one of her own books of poetry that she keeps trying to give you.

old_magazine.title: Old Magazine
old_magazine.flavor: Six months out of date. A page is dog-eared in the back — an article about an avant-garde anthology show in Sweden that you both read in '04 and agreed was the kind of thing you were going to be.

xlr_cable_short.title: XLR · Short
xlr_cable_short.flavor: A two-foot cable. Always exactly the right length when you actually need it.

headphones_spare.title: Spare Headphones
headphones_spare.flavor: Closed-back. The good ones. The pair Marisol uses when she comes up.

cable_labeled_self.title: Cable · labeled 'self'
cable_labeled_self.flavor: A patch cable with a small piece of tape on it. The tape says, in your hand, 'self.' You don't remember labeling it. You don't unplug it.

script_ep_7_reel_2.title: Script · Ep. 7 · Reel 2
script_ep_7_reel_2.flavor: Marisol's script for the Lighthouse Keeper's monologue. Reel 2. The lines she mouths in the picture lock. Some lines have her own handwriting in the margins from '96 — small adjustments she made on the day.
